solution.name: Stress Test 100 Flows
solution.summary:
**Stress Test 100 Flows** (v1.0.0.0) — overall **high risk** posture, driven by the size of the cloned archetype set rather than any single complex flow.

The solution ships 100 cloud flows across 8 architectural archetypes (13× DeepScopes, 13× ParallelBranches, 13× SwitchMany, 13× NestedForeach, 12× UntilLoop, 12× HttpChain, 12× ExpressionsHeavy, 12× RecurrenceAggregator). Total of 1204 actions across the solution; 100 flows roll up to a high impact in at least one category.

**Top three priorities, in order:** (1) Fix the shared-variable race condition in the 13 ParallelBranches flows before any production run — results are non-deterministic. (2) Collapse the 8 cloned archetypes into 8 reusable child flows plus a dispatcher; this is the single biggest maintainability win. (3) Move the literal `https://example.invalid` endpoint to a solution environment variable and document an auth posture on every Request trigger before exposing this solution outside dev.

categories.summary: DeepScopes flows nest Scope 8 levels deep; SwitchMany flows have 12 cases each. Most flows over-use top-level Initialize_Variable. Action counts are individually under 50 but the pattern is duplicated 100×.

Every HTTP action hardcodes https://example.invalid; no connection references or environment variables are used; triggers carry no description. 100 flows clone the same 8 archetypes — consolidate into reusable child flows.
Request triggers ship with no auth posture documented and HTTP bodies interpolate triggerBody() without secureInputs/secureOutputs. No literal secrets, tokens or PII observed.

ParallelBranches flows race on a shared 'results' variable across 6 parallel writers. Foreach lacks explicit concurrency; HTTP calls lack retryPolicy; Until loops set the maximum 50-iteration / 1-hour limit. RecurrenceAggregator filters after a full GET.

topRisks.label: Race condition on shared variable across parallel branches
topRisks.desc: 13 ParallelBranches flows write to a shared 'results' variable from 6 parallel AppendToArrayVariable actions joined at Join_Wait. Variable updates are not transactional across branches; results are non-deterministic under load.

topRisks.label: 100 flows are clones of 8 archetypes
topRisks.desc: The solution ships 13× DeepScopes, 13× ParallelBranches, 13× SwitchMany, 13× NestedForeach, 12× UntilLoop, 12× HttpChain, 12× ExpressionsHeavy, 12× RecurrenceAggregator. Replace with 8 reusable child flows plus a thin dispatcher.

topRisks.label: Excessive Scope nesting (depth 8)
topRisks.desc: 13 DeepScopes flows nest Try_L8 → Try_L7 → … → Try_L1 → DoWork eight levels deep. Refactor the inner work into a child flow and wrap with a single try/catch.

topRisks.label: Hardcoded endpoint in every HTTP action
topRisks.desc: All HTTP actions across HttpChain and RecurrenceAggregator point at the literal 'https://example.invalid/…'. Move endpoints to solution environment variables so the flow promotes cleanly between dev/test/prod.

topRisks.label: Request triggers expose no auth posture
topRisks.desc: All HTTP Request triggers declare no authentication strategy (Entra ID, SAS, IP allow-list). Document and enforce auth in DLP policies before any production exposure.

topRisks.label: Until loops set at the maximum 50-iteration / 1-hour cap
topRisks.desc: 12 UntilLoop flows use limit count=50, timeout=PT1H. Switch to the asynchronous flow pattern with a webhook callback for long-running waits.

topRisks.label: Nested Foreach without explicit concurrency
topRisks.desc: 13 NestedForeach flows nest an Inner Foreach inside an Outer Foreach with neither setting runtimeConfiguration.concurrency. Set a deliberate degree of parallelism so behaviour is predictable.

topRisks.label: Recurrence aggregator filters after a full GET
topRisks.desc: 12 RecurrenceAggregator flows pull the full collection then filter in-engine. Push the predicate to the source (OData $filter or server-side query) to cut transferred bytes and engine time.

items.label: Excessive Scope nesting (depth 8)
items.desc: Flow nests Scope actions 8 levels deep starting at Try_L8. This violates the readability + testability guideline; refactor by promoting inner blocks into a child flow. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/avoid-anti-patterns

items.label: Trigger 'manual' has no description
items.desc: The trigger has no description string. Add a short summary so the next maintainer understands the contract without reading the body. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/use-consistent-naming-conventions

items.label: No connection references / environment variables used
items.desc: Flow uses raw HTTP without a solution-aware connection reference or environment variable. Authoring inside a solution and using connection references is required for safe ALM. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/understand-benefits-solution-aware-flows

items.label: Request trigger 'manual' has no auth posture declared
items.desc: HTTP Request trigger does not document its auth posture (Entra ID, SAS, IP allow-list). Add 'operationOptions': 'IncludeAuthenticationHeader' and restrict access via Power Platform DLP + tenant settings. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/prevent-data-exfiltration

items.label: 6 parallel Compose branches
items.desc: Sibling branches that fan out then join at a single follow-up action. Group inside a Scope for clearer run history. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/avoid-anti-patterns

items.label: Trigger 'manual' has no description

items.label: Race condition on variable 'results' across 6 parallel writers
items.desc: Actions Branch_0_Append, Branch_1_Append, Branch_2_Append all write to variable 'results' from parallel branches. Variable updates are not transactional across branches; results are non-deterministic. Collect per-branch outputs into a Compose and aggregate after the join. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/avoid-anti-patterns

items.label: Switch 'Big_Switch' has 12 cases
items.desc: Large case lists are hard to maintain; consider a lookup table in Dataverse or a child flow per case family. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/leave-complex-business-logic-out

See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/understand-benefits-solution-aware-flows

items.label: Request trigger 'manual' has no auth posture declared
items.desc: HTTP Request trigger does not document its auth posture (Entra ID, SAS, IP allow-list). Add 'operationOptions': 'IncludeAuthenticationHeader' and restrict access via Power Platform DLP + tenant settings. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/prevent-data-exfiltration

items.label: Race condition on variable 'results' across 6 parallel writers
items.desc: Actions Branch_0_Append, Branch_1_Append, Branch_2_Append all write to variable 'results' from parallel branches. Variable updates are not transactional across branches; results are non-deterministic. Collect per-branch outputs into a Compose and aggregate after the join. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/avoid-anti-patterns

items.label: Switch 'Big_Switch' has 12 cases
items.desc: Large case lists are hard to maintain; consider a lookup table in Dataverse or a child flow per case family. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/leave-complex-business-logic-out

items.desc: HTTP actions (List) rely on the default retry. Declare an explicit exponential retryPolicy so transient 429/5xx don't fail the orchestration. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/error-handling

items.label: Filter 'Filter_Active' runs after a full GET
items.desc: The HTTP GET returns the full collection and the flow filters in-engine afterwards. Push the predicate to the source (OData $filter or server-side query) to cut transferred bytes and engine time. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/work-with-relevant-data

items.label: Excessive Scope nesting (depth 8)
items.desc: Flow nests Scope actions 8 levels deep starting at Try_L8. This violates the readability + testability guideline; refactor by promoting inner blocks into a child flow. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/power-automate/guidance/coding-guidelines/avoid-anti-patterns
